proof_stages.label: Orthogonal Shimura varieties and special-cycle generating series
proof_stages.summary: The lecture recalls the lattice of signature (n,2), the period domain D and quotient D/Γ, special divisors Z(m), and the Kudla-Millson theorem that the generating series of their classes on the open Shimura variety is a holomorphic modular form of weight 1+n/2.

proof_stages.label: Boundary-correction problem and Kudla conjecture
proof_stages.summary: Low-dimensional cases show that the open modularity theorem can become trivial or nonmodular after compactification, leading to Kudla’s conjecture that special cycles admit canonical boundary corrections on a smooth projective compactification so that modularity is restored.

proof_stages.label: Baily-Borel boundary and Hodge-theoretic types
proof_stages.summary: The Baily-Borel compactification is described by rational boundary components: type II components from rank-two totally isotropic lattices, each a copy of the upper half-plane, and type III components from rank-one isotropic lines, with an interpretation via degenerations of weight-two Hodge structures.

proof_stages.label: Toroidal compactification and its boundary divisors
proof_stages.summary: A smooth toroidal compactification is introduced: type II boundary divisors are Kuga-Sato-type varieties over modular curves, while type III boundary divisors arise from rays in rational polyhedral cone decompositions of Lorentzian cones and are controlled by toric geometry.

proof_stages.label: Explicit corrected modular generating series
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states an explicit corrected series: Zariski closures of special cycles are amended by E2 times theta series attached to J⊥/J along type II divisors and by a Zagier mock-modular series times theta series attached to γ⊥ along type III divisors, yielding a holomorphic modular form of weight 1+n/2 in Chow in the stated range.

proof_stages.label: Reduction of modularity to testing against cycles
proof_stages.summary: To prove cohomological modularity, the corrected generating series is paired with H2-cycles; cycles supported in the interior reduce to Kudla-Millson modularity, whose intuition is the geometric analogue of classical theta-series modularity via Poisson summation.

proof_stages.label: Boundary intersection formulas and cancellation of nonholomorphic parts
proof_stages.summary: For boundary-supported test cycles, toric geometry expresses intersections of Zariski closures as theta sums of piecewise-linear functions on Lorentzian lattices, whose nonholomorphic modular completions cancel against the completions of the E2 and Zagier correction terms; an exact-sequence/superrigidity argument upgrades the result to Chow.

examples.name: The signature (2,2) product of modular curves
examples.note: For the unimodular lattice U⊕U, the open generating series is trivial but compactification produces an E2 boundary correction, visibly motivating the quasi-modular part of the general correction formula.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker takes the signature (2,2) lattice, identifies the quotient with a product of modular curves, computes the generating series in the interior and compactification, and writes the boundary correction.

passes.reason: The low-rank split signature-(2,2) case decomposes as a product of modular curves and is used to show the uncorrected compactified series is only quasi-modular.

examples.engagement_reason: For a degenerating K3 surface over a punctured disk, the speaker analyzes monodromy, forms N = -log T, distinguishes nilpotent orders, and relates the resulting weight filtrations to type-two and type-three boundary components.

examples.name: Elliptic K3 surfaces with section
